meta.untertitel: How a data gap skews the world for half of humanity
meta.buch: Invisible Women: Data Bias in a World Designed for Men

statistiken.headline: In car crashes, women are 47 % more likely to be seriously injured than men
statistiken.kontext: Crash-test dummies are based on the 50th-percentile man: about 1.77 m tall and 76 kg. A female body is not represented in the standard test.
statistiken.quelle: IMF Finance & Development, review of Invisible Women

statistiken.headline: 71 % higher risk of moderate injury in a crash
statistiken.kontext: Women's seating position, height and muscle distribution are not accounted for in standard safety tests.
statistiken.quelle: Society of Women Engineers / All Together

statistiken.headline: 17 % higher chance of dying in a car crash
statistiken.kontext: This is the result of the same data gap: safety technology such as head restraints, belts and airbags is designed around the average man.
statistiken.quelle: IMF Finance & Development

statistiken.headline: Women's heart attacks are misdiagnosed 50 % more often
statistiken.kontext: Women often don't present the "Hollywood heart attack" with chest and arm pain. Depending on the type, misdiagnosis rises to as much as 59 % (STEMI) or 41 % (NSTEMI).
statistiken.quelle: University of Leeds / MINAP registry, cited in Invisible Women; British Heart Foundation

statistiken.headline: About two thirds of women show "atypical" heart-attack symptoms
statistiken.kontext: Instead of strong chest pain, women often feel pressure, shortness of breath, nausea or abdominal pain. These signs are recognised as a heart attack less often.
statistiken.quelle: All Together / Society of Women Engineers

statistiken.headline: Women take 80 % of medication in the US, yet most testing is done on men
statistiken.kontext: At the same time, fewer than half of the prescription drugs on the US market were studied for sex-specific reactions.
statistiken.quelle: American University, review of Invisible Women

statistiken.headline: In 1977, the US drug regulator excluded women of childbearing age from drug trials

anteil.label: Women's share of clinical trials worldwide (2016)

statistiken.kontext: After the thalidomide scandal, women were kept out of trials for decades. By 2016, their share of clinical trials worldwide was still only around 43 %.
statistiken.quelle: British Journal of General Practice; Oxford Protein Informatics Group

statistiken.headline: PMS affects about 90 % of women, yet erectile dysfunction gets 5× more research
statistiken.kontext: PMS affects roughly 90 % of women but remains under-researched. There are far more studies on erectile dysfunction.
statistiken.quelle: Invisible Women (p. 229), reading notes

statistiken.headline: Women perform about 75 % of the world's unpaid work

anteil.label: Women's share of global unpaid work

statistiken.kontext: Childcare, care work, housework and "mental load" fall mostly to women and appear in no wage statistic.
statistiken.quelle: Invisible Women, Part 2 "The Workplace"

statistiken.headline: Women's unpaid care work is worth about 10 trillion US dollars a year
statistiken.kontext: McKinsey puts the contribution of unpaid care work to annual global GDP at about 10 trillion US dollars. Economically invisible, yet it holds the system together.
statistiken.quelle: McKinsey, cited in Invisible Women

statistiken.headline: Office air conditioning is on average 5 degrees too cold for women
statistiken.kontext: The standard formula is based on the metabolism of a roughly 70 kg, 40-year-old man and overestimates the female metabolic rate by up to 35 %.
statistiken.quelle: Invisible Women (p. 113), quote collection

statistiken.headline: The temperature standard overestimates the female metabolism by up to 35 %
statistiken.kontext: Because the formula is built on a male reference body, women's energy expenditure is set far too high.
statistiken.quelle: Invisible Women, quote collection

statistiken.headline: Google's speech recognition understands men 70 % more reliably
statistiken.kontext: The systems were trained mostly on male voice data, a classic data gap in technology.
statistiken.quelle: Oxford Protein Informatics Group, book review

statistiken.headline: Standard smartphones are too big for the average woman's hand
statistiken.kontext: Display sizes around 5.5 inches are built for men's hands and two-handed use, even though women own smartphones more often.
statistiken.quelle: Invisible Women (p. 159), reading notes

statistiken.headline: In Sweden, 69 % of winter pedestrian injuries were sustained by women

anteil.label: Women's share of winter pedestrian injuries

statistiken.kontext: Main roads, used mainly by commuters (mostly men), were cleared first; footpaths and cycle paths last. Most of the injured were women, often on foot and with a pram.
statistiken.quelle: FSG, Karlskoga case study, Invisible Women, Chapter 1

statistiken.headline: 79 % of all pedestrian injuries happen in winter
statistiken.kontext: Ice on the footpaths cleared last hits people on foot the hardest, women disproportionately. Better clearing even saved money, because hospital costs dropped.
statistiken.quelle: FSG, Karlskoga case study

statistiken.headline: Women in Germany earn 16 % less per hour than men
statistiken.kontext: The unadjusted gender pay gap was 16 % in 2024. Women earned 22.24 € gross per hour on average, men 26.34 €.
statistiken.quelle: Federal Statistical Office (Destatis), press release no. 056/2025

statistiken.headline: Women's pensions in the EU are 24.5 % lower than men's
statistiken.kontext: In 2024, women aged 65 and over in the EU received on average 24.5 % less pension than men. The gap ranges from 5.6 % in Estonia to 40.3 % in Malta.
statistiken.quelle: Eurostat, Gender Pension Gap 2024

statistiken.headline: Women do about 44 % more unpaid care work than men
statistiken.kontext: According to the 2022 time-use survey, women did about 9 hours more unpaid work per week than men. The gender care gap was 44.3 %.
statistiken.quelle: Federal Statistical Office (Destatis), Time Use Survey 2022 (PR no. 073/2024)

statistiken.headline: Only 24.7 % of DAX executive board seats are held by women

anteil.label: DAX board seats held by women

statistiken.kontext: As of 1 September 2024, women held 24.7 % of executive board seats at the 40 largest DAX companies, second to last by international comparison.
statistiken.quelle: AllBright Foundation, Autumn Report 2024

statistiken.headline: On average it takes 6.6 years to diagnose endometriosis
statistiken.kontext: A 2024 scoping review by the University of York found an average diagnostic delay of 6.6 years, ranging from a few months to 27 years.
statistiken.quelle: University of York, scoping review on endometriosis diagnosis, 2024

statistiken.headline: Only 27.5 % of all parliamentary seats worldwide are held by women

anteil.label: Parliamentary seats worldwide held by women

statistiken.kontext: As of 1 January 2026, women hold 27.5 % of national parliamentary seats worldwide, up just 0.3 percentage points from the year before.
statistiken.quelle: Inter-Parliamentary Union (IPU), Women in Parliament 2026

statistiken.headline: At the current pace it will take 123 years to reach global gender parity
statistiken.kontext: Worldwide, the gender gap is only 68.8 % closed. At today's rate of progress, full parity would not be reached for another 123 years.
statistiken.quelle: World Economic Forum, Global Gender Gap Report 2025
